rule: 100 inside 18-26 C monthly mean maximum comfort band; subtract 8 points per degree outside the band; clamp 0-100.
interpretation: Higher values mean milder daytime temperatures.

rule: 100 inside 10-19 C monthly mean minimum comfort band; subtract 8 points per degree outside the band; clamp 0-100.
interpretation: Higher values mean more comfortable nights for sleeping.
Higher values mean fewer hot and extreme-hot days.
Higher values mean lower everyday rain burden.

rule: Average 9am and 3pm relative humidity. 100 inside 40-62%; subtract 2.2 points per percentage point outside the band; clamp 0-100.
interpretation: Higher values mean less muggy or overly dry air.

rule: Linearly scale monthly mean daily solar exposure from 7-26 MJ/m2 to 0-100; clamp 0-100.
interpretation: Higher values mean more available sunshine/solar exposure.

input_fields: day_temp_score, night_temp_score, heat_score, rain_score, humidity_score, sunshine_score
rule: Weighted average using balanced weights renormalised after excluding annual stability.
interpretation: A month-level comfort score used to show seasonal rhythm and stability.

rule: 100 - 1.8*(max monthly comfort - min monthly comfort); clamp 0-100.
interpretation: Higher values mean comfort is more stable across the year.

input_fields: seven component scores and scenario weights
rule: Weighted average of annual component scores under each transparent preference scenario.
interpretation: Author-derived comparative index, not an official climate ranking.
